A Curriculum Built for Real Employment

Every track is designed around the skills our employer partners actually need.

Prerequisite

The TEAL+ Foundation

Before applying to the MCRI, students attend a MATTER Innovation Hub where they are immersed in the TEAL+ model — technology-enabled active learning. They arrive as confident, collaborative, and curious learners.

Learn more about TEAL+

Our Approach

At the MCRI, essential skills are not separate from the technical curriculum — they are the foundation upon which all technical learning is built. We don't teach empathy after we teach Jamf Pro. We build students who lead with empathy, and then we give them Jamf Pro.

Every course is designed to be applied immediately. Students don't learn in isolation — they practice their skills in context, in teams, on real problems. When an employer partner meets an MCRI graduate, they're not meeting someone who has passed a test. They're meeting someone who has worked.

Core Curriculum Tracks

Six tracks, each designed around the skills our employer partners actually need.

Apple Platform Fundamentals

Students build foundational knowledge of Apple platforms, hardware, and the Apple ecosystem — developing the skills that underpin everything else in the curriculum.

  • Apple Teacher certification
  • Everyone Can Code curriculum completion

Swift Development

From Swift fundamentals to building and shipping real iOS apps. Students progress through Apple's full Learn to Code sequence and earn the App Development with Swift credential.

  • Learn to Code 1, 2, and 3
  • Swift Explorations, Fundamentals, and Data Collections
  • App Development with Swift — Associate & Certified User
  • Design and build their own app using Swift

Jamf

Hands-on training in enterprise Apple device management using Jamf Pro and Jamf Protect — the exact tools students will use on day one in their employer partner roles.

  • Jamf Certified Associate — Jamf Pro
  • Jamf Certified Associate — Jamf Protect
  • Command Line Innovator badge

Essential Skills

Empathy, collaboration, problem-solving, and professional communication. These skills are not an add-on — they are the foundation every technical skill is built upon.

  • Completing Leadershift
  • Completing Getting Things Done
  • Present on incorporating these skills into daily life

Agentic AI

Students learn to work alongside AI tools as a professional multiplier — using agentic AI to accelerate development, solve problems, and deliver higher-quality work in less time.

  • Completing Anthropic's Claude Code in Action
  • Earning Certificate of Completion

macOS Fundamentals

Deep familiarity with macOS as both a user environment and an administration target — essential for supporting the Apple-first workplaces of every MCRI employer partner.

  • Navigate macOS completion

Additional Technical Pathways

Based on employer needs and team availability, students may also explore:

  • Web Services
  • Agile & SCRUM
  • Web Accessibility
  • SQL
  • UX/UI

Delivered by industry professionals

Courses are delivered in person by industry professionals and are paired with certifications to validate student learning. Throughout the program, students continuously apply both technical and essential skills in collaborative, real-world contexts.

Apprenticeship Model

Not an internship.
A direct path to employment.

After completing the curriculum, students transition into a six-month apprenticeship with an employer partner, paid for by Jamf Community Education Initiatives.

  • Work as a full team member, contributing from day one
  • Apply both technical and essential skills in real-world contexts
  • Paid for by Jamf Community Education Initiatives
  • Direct path to a full-time remote role

The MCRI Professional Badge

Students who successfully complete the curriculum and are offered a full-time role with an employer partner earn the MCRI Professional badge, signifying both technical readiness and strong foundational professional skills.

Ready to partner with us?

Become an Employer Partner

A post-secondary technology institution in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe — with a direct pathway to full-time employment.
